If noise removal was turned on, it stays on; if it was turned off, it stays off. For other devices, the noise removal state returns to the default state (on) when a call is ended, or you restart the device.

If noise removal was turned on, it stays on; if it was turned off, it stays off. For other devices, the noise removal state returns to the default state (off) when a call is ended, or you restart the device.

description.Auto: The camera brightness is automatically adjusted by the device. The Cameras Camera Brightness DefaultLevel setting has no effect.
description.Manual: Enable manual control of the camera brightness level. The brightness level is set using the Cameras Camera Brightness DefaultLevel setting.

description.Auto: If the camera detects that it is mounted upside down, the image is automatically flipped. If the camera cannot auto-detect whether it is mounted upside down or not, the image is not changed. As an example, the Sony PTZ 4K camera cannot auto-detect that it's upside down. If you mount it upside down, you must set this parameter to On.
description.Off: Display the image on screen the normal way.
description.On: Display the image flipped upside down. This setting is used when a camera is mounted upside down, but cannot automatically detect which way it is mounted.

description.Auto: A Quad Camera will do single shot auto focusing once a call is connected and when the view has changed. A Precision 60 or PTZ 4K camera will do continuous auto focusing. It will run auto focus once a call is connected, after pan, tilt, zoom have changed, and generally when the camera identifies a change in the scene.
description.AutoLimited: Precision 60 camera. In most cases, this mode will operate the same way as Auto. In some cases, AutoLimited will reduce the focus hunting that may occur.
description.Manual: Turn the autofocus off and adjust the camera focus manually.

description.Off: Disables the AutoFraming feature.

description.Auto: The system automatically assigns the camera's role.
description.Manual: The camera is not part of a multicamera speakertrack experience.
description.SpeakerTrackPrimary: The camera is designated as the primary speakertrack camera in a multicamera setup. Only one camera should be configured as the primary.
description.SpeakerTrackSecondary: The camera is designated as a secondary speakertrack camera in a multicamera setup.

description.Auto: The system decides whether camera [n] should be used for speaker tracking best overview framing.
description.Off: Camera [n] will not be used by speaker tracking for best overview framing.
description.On: Camera [n] will be used by speaker tracking for best overview framing.

description.Disabled: When the device enters standby mode, no power-off command is sent to the camera. Upon exiting standby, the camera remains in its previous state without any additional movement, making it immediately available for use.
description.Enabled: When the device enters standby mode, power-off is sent to the camera. Upon exiting standby, power-on is issued, causing the camera to perform a calibration process. During calibration, the camera pans to its maximum position and then returns to the center, which takes approximately 12 seconds.

description.Auto: If the camera detects that it is mounted upside down, the image is automatically flipped. If the camera cannot auto-detect whether it is mounted upside down or not, the image is not changed. As an example, the Sony PTZ 4K camera cannot auto-detect that it's upside down. If you mount it upside down, you must set this parameter to On.
description.Off: Display the image on screen the normal way.
description.On: Display the image flipped upside down. This setting is used when a camera is mounted upside down, but cannot automatically detect which way it is mounted.

description.Auto: A Precision 60 or PTZ 4K camera will do continuous auto focusing. It will run auto focus once a call is connected, after pan, tilt, zoom have changed, and generally when the camera identifies a change in the scene. The integrated camera will do single shot auto focusing once a call is connected and when the view has changed.
description.Manual: Turn the autofocus off and adjust the camera focus manually.

description.Auto: Auto is the default and the recommended setting.
description.Manual: In manual mode the gamma value is changed with the gamma level setting, ref. Cameras Camera [n] Gamma Level.

description.Auto: The devices switches between Day and Night mode automatically based on changes in brightness. Refer to the IrCutFilter Threshold setting how to control when to switch between the two modes.
description.Day: The IR cut filter is on.
description.Night: The IR cut filter is off.

description.Auto: If the camera detects that it is mounted upside down, the image is automatically mirrored. If the camera cannot auto-detect whether it is mounted upside down or not, the image is not changed.
description.Off: Display the image as other people see you.
description.On: Display the image as you see yourself in a mirror.

description.Disabled: When the device enters standby mode, no power-off command is sent to the camera. Upon exiting standby, the camera remains in its previous state without any additional movement, making it immediately available for use.
description.Enabled: When the device enters standby mode, power-off is sent to the camera. Upon exiting standby, power-on is issued, causing the camera to perform a calibration process. During calibration, the camera pans to its maximum position and then returns to the center, which takes approximately 12 seconds.

description.True: The PresenterTrack feature is available for use.

description.Auto: Audio tracking from table microphones is used as needed.
description.Off: Audio tracking from table microphones is disabled.

description.Auto: The device will zoom in either solely on the active speaker or the active speaker and the participants in close proximity to him/her (group frame).
description.Off: The device will keep all the persons in the room in the camera framing at all times.
description.On: The device will zoom in on the active speaker or the group frame.

description.Auto: Automatically detect which video inputs the cameras are connected to.
description.Manual: Manually define which video inputs the cameras are connected to. Use the Cameras SpeakerTrack ConnectorDetection CameraLeft and Cameras SpeakerTrack ConnectorDetection CameraRight settings.

description.Auto: The same as BestOverview.
description.BestOverview: The default framing mode is Best overview.
description.Current: The framing mode is kept unchanged when leaving a call.
description.Manual: The camera is controlled manually. The pan-tilt-zoom values are kept unchanged when leaving a call.

description.Auto: The default framing mode is Dynamic.
description.BestOverview: The default framing mode is Best overview.
description.Closeup: The default framing mode is Closeup (speaker tracking).
description.Current: The framing mode is kept unchanged when leaving a call.
description.Dynamic: The default framing mode is Dynamic.
description.Frames: The default framing mode is Frames.
description.GroupAndSpeaker: The default framing mode is GroupAndSpeaker.
description.Manual: The camera is controlled manually. The pan-tilt-zoom values are kept unchanged when leaving a call.

description.Auto: The default framing mode is Dynamic.
description.BestOverview: The default framing mode is Best overview.
description.Closeup: The default framing mode is Closeup (speaker tracking).
description.Current: The framing mode is kept unchanged when leaving a call.
description.Dynamic: The default framing mode is Dynamic.
description.Frames: The default framing mode is Frames.
description.Manual: The camera is controlled manually. The pan-tilt-zoom values are kept unchanged when leaving a call.

description.Auto: The user can switch Frames on and off using the camera controls on the device's user interface.
description.Off: Frames are not available.

description.MultiRow: arranges individually framed people across two rows, providing a double-row grid layout.
description.SingleRow: creates a grid layout with a single horizontal row of grid cells, each containing either groups of people or individual participants.

description.Auto: Group and Speaker mode is enabled.
description.Off: Group and Speaker mode is disabled.

description.Extended: The longest range. This applies to a rectangle centered in the camera field-of-view. This rectangle is visualized in the Speakertrack diagnostics view.

description.Auto: As long as a meeting zone is defined, it will be used. If a meeting zone is not defined, the behavior is the same as Off.
description.Off: The meeting zone feature is not used, regardless of whether a meeting zone is defined or not.

description.Auto: Best overview is switched on. The device will detect people in the room and automatically select the best camera framing. Users can switch best overview on or off instantly in the camera control panel on the touch controller, but the feature is switched back on after each call so that the device is ready for the next user.
description.Off: Best overview is switched off.

description.Auto: Speaker tracking is switched on. The device will detect people in the room and automatically select the best camera framing. Users can switch speaker track on or off instantly in the camera control panel on the touch controller, but the feature is switched back on after each call so that the device is ready for the next user.
description.Off: Speaker tracking is switched off. If you have a SpeakerTrack 60 Camera the two cameras operate as individual cameras.

description.Auto: Speaker tracking is switched on. The device will detect people in the room and automatically select the best camera framing. Users can switch speaker track on or off instantly in the camera control panel on the touch controller, but the feature is switched back on after each call so that the device is ready for the next user.
description.Off: Speaker tracking is switched off.

description.Auto: This is the normal tracking mode.
description.Conservative: The camera view will change to a new speaker later than in normal tracking mode.

description.3x: Maximum zoom factor for automatic framing features is 3x.
description.4x: Maximum zoom factor for automatic framing features is 4x.
description.Auto: Our recommended zoom range.

description.3x: Maximum zoom factor for automatic framing features is 3x.
description.4x: Maximum zoom factor for automatic framing features is 4x.
description.5x: Maximum zoom factor for automatic framing features is 5x.
description.Auto: Our recommended zoom range.

description.Off: You can answer incoming calls manually by tapping Answer on the touch controller.
description.On: The device automatically answers incoming calls, except if you are already in a call. You can answer or decline incoming calls manually when you are already engaged in a call.

description.Off: The incoming call will not be muted.
description.On: The incoming call will be muted when automatically answered.

description.Auto: Enables auto-selection of the call protocol based on which protocols are available. If multiple protocols are available, the order of priority is: 1) SIP; 2) H323. If the device cannot register, the auto-selection chooses H323.
description.H323: All calls are set up as H.323 calls.
description.Sip: All calls are set up as SIP calls.
description.Spark: All calls are set up via the Webex infrastructure.

description.Off: notifications about network issues and degraded call quality will not be shown.
description.On: notifications about network issues and degraded call quality will be shown.

description.Off: Show only the video streams; no information overlay.
description.On: Show the transparent data layer on top of the video streams.

description.False: People can see and interact with embedded apps on the device.
description.True: People will see a notification when an embedded app is shared. They can't see or interact with the app on the device; they must use a meeting client.

description.BestEffort: The device will use encryption whenever possible.
description.Off: The device will not use encryption.
description.On: The device will only allow calls that are encrypted.

description.Auto: The far end is allowed to select video sources or control the camera only when speaker tracking is disabled. Far end control is not allowed on personal mode devices.
description.Off: The far end can't select video sources or control the camera.
description.On: The far end is allowed to select video sources and control the camera on all devices (both personal and shared). Speaker tracking will be disabled when the device receives the first FarEndControl Camera Move command, and then re-enabled when the call ends.

description.Off: Disable the far end control signal capability.
description.On: Enable the far end control signal capability.

description.Off: It is not possible to send messages between two devices.
description.On: It is possible to send messages between two devices in a point-to-point call.

description.Active: Hide participants whose video is off during meetings, regardless of the usage in the previous call.
description.Auto: Auto will resolve to "Active" at the beginning of calls for shared devices, and to "Previous" for personal devices.
description.Inactive: Show participants whose video is off during meetings, regardless of the usage in previous call.
description.Previous: Previous will set the same state as the last call.

description.Auto: Show notifications only for the first 25 joining participants.

description.Off: If muted during a call, let the microphones remain muted after the call is disconnected.
description.On: Unmute the microphones after the call is disconnected.

description.Auto: The multipoint method is selected automatically. Multiparty conferences are set up using the built-in MultiSite feature, provided that the MultiSite option key is installed on the device, and that you are calling another device (not an MCU). Only the MultiSite host can add participants. This prevents cascaded conferences. If the device doesn't have the MultiSite option key, you cannot call more than one device on video. When calling another video device (not an MCU), some devices support adding one extra participant on audio-only.
description.CUCMMediaResourceGroupList: Multiparty conferences are hosted by the CUCM configured conference bridge. This setting is provisioned by CUCM in a CUCM environment and should never be set manually by the user.
description.MultiSite: Multiparty conferences are set up using the built-in MultiSite feature, provided that the MultiSite option key is installed on the device. If the device doesn't have the MultiSite option key, you cannot call more than one device on video. Some devices support adding one extra participant on audio-only.
description.Off: You cannot call more than one device on video. Multiparty conferences may be set up via an MCU if you call an MCU that allows devices to add participants to a conference (Direct Remote Add). When calling another video device (not an MCU), some devices support adding one extra participant on audio-only.

description.Disabled: Stage synchronization is disabled.
description.Enabled: Stage synchronization is enabled.

description.Auto: Multistream will be used when the conference infrastructure supports the feature. Minimum versions required: CMS 2.2, CUCM 11.5, VCS X8.7.
description.Off: Multistream is disabled. This does not have any effect for devices registered to the Webex cloud service, because these always use multistream. The same applies if your device is linked to Webex Edge for devices and the Webex optimized experience is enabled (that is, joining meetings through the Webex calling infrastructure).

description.Active: People focus is on when you join a meeting.
description.Auto: Like Active on shared mode devices; like Previous for personal mode devices.
description.Inactive: People focus is off when you join a meeting.
description.Previous: When joining a new meeting, the initial people focus state (on or off) is the same as when you left the previous meeting.

description.PreferIpv4: The call protocol will use IPv4.
description.PreferIpv6: The call protocol will use IPv6. This value requires NAT64 and DNS64 in the customer's network.

description.Disabled: Users don't have access to their OneDrive files and whiteboards from the device.
description.Enabled: Users can sign in and access their OneDrive files and store whiteboards.

description.Disabled: the option is not available.

description.InputAcceptAllCalls: When the pin transitions from High to Low on an incoming call, the call will be accepted (the Call Accept command is issued automatically).
description.InputDisconnectAllCalls: When the pin transitions from High to Low during a call, call(s) will be disconnected (the Call Disconnect command is issued automatically).
description.InputMuteMicrophones: When the pin transitions from High to Low during a call, the microphones will be muted. When the pin transitions from Low to High during a call, the microphones will be unmuted (the Audio Mute and Audio Unmute commands are issued automatically).
description.InputNoAction: The pin state does not trigger any action. The pin state can be read with the GPIO Pin [1..4] State status.
description.OutputAllCallsEncrypted: The pin is set Low when all calls are encrypted, and High when one or more calls are not encrypted.
description.OutputInCall: The pin is set Low when in call, and High when not in call.
description.OutputManualState: The pin state can be set to High or Low by the GPIO ManualState Set PinX command. It is set to +12 V or 0 V, respectively.
description.OutputMicrophonesMuted: The pin is set Low when the microphones are muted, and High when they are not muted.
description.OutputPresentationOn: The pin is set Low when a presentation is active, and High when a presentation is not active.
description.OutputStandbyActive: The pin is set Low when the device is in standby mode, and High when the device is not in standby mode.

description.Off: The device will not try to authenticate itself to a H.323 Gatekeeper, but will still try a normal registration.
description.On: If an H.323 Gatekeeper indicates that it requires authentication, the device will try to authenticate itself to the gatekeeper. Requires the H323 Authentication LoginName and H323 Authentication Password settings to be defined on both the device and the Gatekeeper.

description.Direct: You can only make an H.323 call by dialing an IP address directly.
description.Gatekeeper: The device uses a Gatekeeper to make an H.323 call. When choosing this option, the H323 Gatekeeper Address must also be configured.

description.Off: The device only supports 128-bit AES encryption keys.
description.On: The device supports both 128-bit and 256-bit AES encryption keys.

description.Auto: Auto mode works only if you have specified the NAT address in the H323 NAT Address setting.
description.Off: NAT is turned off, and the H323 NAT Address setting will be ignored. In this case you will not be able to set up a call to a device that is outside of your private network unless you use a gatekeeper.
description.On: NAT is always turned on. You must specify the NAT address in the H323 NAT Address setting. The device will always signal the H323 NAT Address instead of its private IP address in Q.931 and H.245. If the H323 NAT Address is wrong or not set, H.323 calls cannot be set up.

description.False: The video device can communicate only over HTTPS.
description.True: The video device can communicate over both HTTPS and HTTP.

description.False: The device always checks that the HTTPS server has a valid certificate. No communication with the server takes place if the certificate validation fails.
description.True: The device is allowed to skip the certificate validation process before communicating with the server.

description.Off: The video device cannot communicate with an external HTTP(S) server.
description.On: The video device is allowed to communicate with an external HTTP(S) server.

description.Off: Set up communication directly with the server (not using a proxy).
description.On: Set up communication via proxy.

description.Off: The device doesn't check the certificate of the HTTPS server.
description.On: The device checks if the certificate of the HTTPS server can be trusted. If not, the connection between the device and the server is not established.

description.Off: Set up communication directly with the server (not using a proxy).
description.On: Set up communication via proxy.

description.Off: Logs from the device can not be uploaded to the Webex cloud.
description.On: Logs from the device can be uploaded to the Webex cloud.

description.Off: Device logs will not be stored on the remote syslog server.
description.On: Device logs will be stored on the remote syslog server.

description.Off: The device doesn't check the certificate of the syslog server.
description.On: The device checks if the certificate of the syslog server can be trusted. If not, the connection between the device and the server is not established.

description.Off: System logs will not be stored on the device.
description.On: System logs will be stored on the device.

description.Off: Disable the system logging service.
description.On: Enable the system logging service.

description.Off: The macro runtime will not start automatically after a restart of the device.
description.On: The macro runtime will start automatically after a restart of the device.

description.Off: Don't expose macro logged errors as diagnostics.
description.On: Expose macro logged errors as diagnostics.

description.False: Babel transpiling is disabled. Macros containing old style "require", e.g. const xapi = require("xapi") will fail. Only e.g. import xapi from "xapi" will work.
description.True: Babel transpiling is enabled. The macro runtime engine accepts old style "require", e.g. const xapi = require("xapi")

description.External: The device sends the audit logs to an external syslog server. The syslog server must support UDP.
description.ExternalSecure: The device sends encrypted audit logs to an external syslog server that is verified by a certificate in the Audit CA list. The Audit CA list file must be uploaded to the device using the web interface. The common_name parameter of a certificate in the CA list must match the IP address or DNS name of the syslog server, and the secure TCP server must be set up to listen for secure (TLS) TCP Syslog messages.
description.Internal: The device records the audit logs to internal logs, and rotates logs when they are full.
description.Off: No audit logging is performed.

description.Halt: If a halt condition is detected the device is rebooted and only the auditor is allowed to operate the unit until the halt condition has passed. When the halt condition has passed the audit logs are re-spooled to the syslog server. Halt conditions are: A network breach (no physical link), no syslog server running (or incorrect address or port to the syslog server), TLS authentication failed (if in use), local backup (re-spooling) log full.
description.Ignore: The device will continue its normal operation, and rotate internal logs when full. When the connection is restored it will again send its audit logs to the syslog server.

description.Auto: Will use UDP port number 514 when the Security Audit Logging Mode is set to External. Will use TCP port number 6514 when the Security Audit Logging Mode is set to ExternalSecure.
description.Manual: Will use the port value defined in the Security Audit Server Port setting.

description.Auto: When set to Auto, the device uses either the Legacy or RFC5922 method of certificate verification. A given software version will always use only one of the methods. The method used may change between different software versions.
description.Legacy: When set to Legacy, the device uses the old verification method. It compares a list of names, including DNS SRV records with those in the received certificate, without applying RFC5922 standards.
description.RFC5922: When set to RFC5922, the device compares the hostname of the dialed URI (e.g., webex.com or zoomcrc.com) with the Subject Alternative Names (SANs) or Common Name (CN) in the X.509 Certificate, following the rules in RFC5922. RFC5922 provides guidelines for how to extract the identity of a SIP domain from a received certificate and how to use that identity for SIP domain authentication.

description.AutoShare: While in a call, the content on the video input will automatically be presented to the far end as well as on the local screen when you connect the cable, or when the source is activated otherwise (for example when a connected computer wakes up from sleep mode). You do not have to select Share on the user interface. If a presentation source is already connected when you make or answer a call, you have to manually select Share on the user interface.
description.Desktop: The content on the video input will be presented on the screen when you connect the cable, or when the source is activated otherwise (for example when a connected computer wakes up from sleep mode). This applies both when idle and in a call. Also, the content on the video input will stay on the screen when you leave the call, provided that it was the active input at the time of leaving.
description.Manual: The content on the video input will not be presented on the screen until you select Share from the user interface.
description.OnConnect: The content on the video input will be presented on screen when you connect the cable, or when the source is activated otherwise (for example when a connected computer wakes up from sleep mode). Otherwise, the behavior is the same as in manual mode.

description.Desktop: The content on the video input will be presented on the screen when you connect the cable, or when the source is activated otherwise (for example when a connected computer wakes up from sleep mode). This applies both when idle and in a call. Also, the content on the video input will stay on the screen when you leave the call, provided that it was the active input at the time of leaving.
description.Manual: The content on the video input will not be presented on the screen until you select Share from the user interface.
description.OnConnect: The content on the video input will be presented on screen when you connect the cable, or when the source is activated otherwise (for example when a connected computer wakes up from sleep mode). Otherwise, the behavior is the same as in manual mode.

description.Auto: As a starting point Auto resolves to Sharpness and the video stream will be encoded accordingly. The device will continuously analyze the incoming video stream, and if motion is detected it will automatically change to encoding for Motion.
description.Motion: Gives the highest possible frame rate. Used when there is a need for higher frame rates, typically when there is motion in the picture.
description.Sharpness: Gives the highest possible resolution. Used when you want the highest quality of detailed images and graphics.

description.Auto: The number of screens connected to the device is automatically detected, and the layout is distributed on the screens according to the monitor role.
description.Dual: The layout is distributed on screens with monitor role First and Second. If a presentation is part of the layout, all participants in the call are shown on the screen with monitor role First, and the presentation is shown on the screen with monitor role Second.
description.DualPresentationOnly: All participants in the call are shown on the screen with monitor role First. If a presentation is part of the layout, the presentation is shown on the screen with monitor role Second.
description.Single: The same layout is shown on all screens.

description.Single: The same layout is shown on all screens.
description.Triple: The layout is distributed on screens with monitor role First, Second and Third. Screens with monitor role Second are used first, then First, and finally Third. description.TriplePresentationOnly: All participants in the call are distributed on screens with monitor role First and Second. Screens with monitor role Second are used first, then First. If a presentation is part of the layout, the presentation is shown on the screen with monitor role Third.

description.TriplePresentationOnly: All participants in the call are distributed on screens with monitor role First and Second. Screens with monitor role Second are used first, then First. If a presentation is part of the layout, the presentation is shown on the screen with monitor role Third.

description.DetectingFormat: The device has detected an input signal, and is in the process of detecting its format.
description.NotFound: The device doesn't detect any input signal. Typically, this is the case when no cable is connected, or the connected source doesn't output video.
description.OK: The device detects a valid video input signal and the signal format support is reported in the Video Input Source [n] FormatStatus status.
description.Unstable: The device detects an unstable signal. Typically, this can be the case for a few seconds after you have connected the cable, that is, while the hardware tries to detect the video format. This value will also be returned when the device is not able to determine the signal format, or the signal format is inconsistent.

description.Off: Video has not been stopped.
description.On: Video has been stopped.

description.Started: Miracast is enabled, and a direct Wi-Fi connection between the two devices is, or can be, established. The device emits a Miracast beacon.
description.Stopped: Miracast is disabled, and a direct Wi-Fi connection between the two devices is therefore not possible. The device doesn't emit a Miracast beacon

description.Direct: Content is being shared using Wi-Fi Direct.
description.Infrastructure: Content is being shared using the company's network infrastructure (using the MS-MICE protocol).
description.None: Content is currently not being shared.

description.Busy: The video input source is actively in use.
description.Idle: The video input source is not in use.

description.Error: There is an error with the video input format. This can be the case when the video input signal is unstable (SignalState: Unstable) or the video input signal format cannot be determined (and none of the other error states can be used).
description.Interlaced: The video input format is interlaced, which isn't supported. The device support only progressive formats. Not all video input connectors can report this type of error.
description.NotFound: There is no video format present on the specified input. See the Video Input Connector [n] SignalState status for information why it might be missing.
description.Ok: The video input format is supported. The resolution and framerate is reported in Video Input Source [n] Resolution status.
description.OutOfRange: The video input format has higher resolution and/or framerate than the device supports for the specified input. Not all video input connectors can report this type of error.

description.Dual: The layout is distributed on two monitors.
description.DualPresentationOnly: All participants in the call will be shown on the first monitor, while the presentation (if any) will be shown on the second monitor.
description.Single: The same layout is shown on all monitors.
description.Triple: The layout is distributed on three monitors.
description.TriplePresentationOnly: All participants in the call will be shown on the first and second monitors, while the presentation (if any) will be shown on the third monitor.

description.Authenticated: An HDCP link is established, and the link is stable. The connector can transmit HDCP protected content.
description.Inactive: The connector may support HDCP but is currently configured not to allow HDCP protected content (refer to the Video Output Connector [n] HDCPPolicy setting).
description.Unauthenticated: The connector is set up to allow HDCP protected content, but either there is no screen connected to it, or the device is in the process of establishing a link toward the screen. If the link is unstable, the device may remain in this state or revert to Inactive.
description.Unsupported: The connector doesn't support HDCP, and it can't be set up to support it.
